commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Unlearned. He answers, (Jansenius) or rather continues the objections. (Geier) (Calmet)

commentaries.author: Gregory the Wonderworker
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh For there is no remembrance of the wise more than of the fool forever; seeing that which now is in the days to come shall all be forgotten. And how does the wise man die. as the fool. Gregory the Wonderworker A wise person and a foolish person have nothing in common, either in terms of human remembrance or in terms of divine recompense. As for human works, the end already overtakes them all while they still seem to be beginning. But a wise person never shares the same fate as a stupid person.
